name: Festive Peppermint Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the fruit cocktail and reserve the liquid for other uses.
2. Stir the peppermint extract into the drained fruit.
3. Whip the cream until stiff peaks form.
4. Fold the fruit mixture into the whipped cream.
5. Spread half of the fruit mixture into a cooled, baked pastry shell.
6. Freeze the pie until firm.
7. Refrigerate the other half of the fruit mixture.
8. In a medium saucepan, combine butter, chocolate, sugar, evaporated milk, and marshmallows. Heat, stirring continuously, until melted and thickened. Stir in vanilla; cool.
9. Cover one half of the fruit mixture with the chocolate mixture. Freeze until set.
10. Repeat layers once more, freezing after each addition.
11. Freeze the completed pie overnight.
Meringue:
3 egg whites, at room temperature
1/2 teaspoon vanilla
1/4 teaspoon cream of tartar
6 tablespoons granulated sugar
1/4 cup crushed peppermint candy
12. Beat the egg whites with vanilla and cream of tartar until soft peaks form.
13. Add sugar one tablespoon at a time, beating until stiff and glossy peaks are evident and sugar is dissolved.
14. Fold in 3 tablespoons crushed peppermint candy.
15. Remove the pie from the freezer.
16. Spread the meringue over the chocolate layer, sealing it to the crust.
17. Sprinkle the top with remaining peppermint candy.
18. Bake on a cutting board at 475°F (245°C) for 5-6 minutes, or until golden. Serve immediately.
